mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 6.5% over 3 months signals weakening labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings prospects deteriorate.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in inflation expectations (CPIAUCSL) and rising bond yields (FEDFUNDS) signal a shift toward tighter monetary policy, creating headwinds for equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL < 265 and FEDFUNDS > 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates a supply shock, causing stagflationary pressures that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO < 100 and CPIAUCSL > 270.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer demand, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where HOUST > 1400 and RSAFS > 600,000.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) paired with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, increasing wage pressures and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of instances where PAYEMS > 150k and UNRATE < 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in GDP growth (GDPC1) reflects expanding economic activity, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO) during a period of stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) suggests a supply-side shock, creating uncertainty and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO < 95 and CPIAUCSL < 270.
